courses.title: Data Structures and Algorithms
courses.description: Study of data organization and algorithms.

courses.title: Integrated Systems
courses.description: Combining multiple systems into one solution.

lessons.title: What is Programming?
content: Programming is the process of creating instructions that tell a computer how to perform tasks.
Programs are written using programming languages such as Java, Python, and JavaScript.

lessons.title: Programming Languages
content: Programming languages provide a way for developers to communicate with computers.
Different languages are designed for different purposes such as web development, mobile apps, and data analysis.

lessons.title: Variables
content: Variables are named storage locations used to hold data values in memory.
A variable can store different types of data such as numbers, text, and boolean values.

lessons.title: Primitive Data Types
content: Primitive data types are the basic building blocks of data in Java.
Examples include int, double, char, and boolean.

lessons.title: Introduction to Arrays
content: An array is a collection of elements stored in contiguous memory locations.
Arrays allow multiple values of the same type to be stored under a single variable name.

lessons.title: Linked List Operations
content: A linked list consists of nodes where each node points to the next node.
Common operations include insertion, deletion, traversal, and searching.

lessons.title: Bubble Sort
content: Bubble Sort repeatedly compares adjacent elements and swaps them when necessary.
The algorithm continues until the entire list is sorted.

lessons.title: Selection Sort
content: Selection Sort repeatedly finds the smallest element and places it in its correct position.
The algorithm divides the list into sorted and unsorted portions.

lessons.title: HTML Document Structure
content: Every HTML document begins with a DOCTYPE declaration followed by html, head, and body tags.
The structure helps browsers correctly render web pages.

lessons.title: HTML Elements
content: HTML elements are the building blocks of web pages.
Examples include headings, paragraphs, links, images, and tables.

lessons.title: Selectors and Properties
content: CSS selectors target HTML elements that should receive specific styles.
Properties such as color, font-size, and margin define the appearance of elements.

lessons.title: Box Model
content: The CSS box model consists of content, padding, border, and margin.
Understanding the box model is essential for creating proper layouts.

lessons.title: Requirements Gathering
content: Requirements gathering is the process of collecting information from stakeholders.
The goal is to understand the needs and expectations of the system.

lessons.title: Use Case Diagrams
content: Use case diagrams visualize interactions between users and a system.
They help identify system functionality from the user's perspective.

lessons.title: API Integration
content: API integration allows different systems to communicate and exchange data.
Developers use endpoints to send requests and receive responses.

lessons.title: System Deployment
content: System deployment is the process of making an application available to users.
Deployment often includes server configuration, testing, and monitoring.
